messages.content: Hypothetical 2024 Episode of  ItCrowd -   Ray and Moss are remote working, having to deal with IT support for VPN's, combating phishing calls and dealing with "Zoombie" calls.  Jen still seems able to talk even if it's just to a webcam..    A main plot involves them using AI to filter out the  innane queries , with the typical "have you tried turning it off and on again?" routine now in a robotic voice.  A side plot involves Jen using online hypnotherapy to finally quit her smoking habit. ...

Title: "AI Support and Zoombie Mayhem"

Opening Scene: The familiar chaotic IT office is strangely quiet, with Roy (not Ray) and Moss's desks adorned with multiple monitors, each showing different remote support sessions. They're wearing headsets, juggling calls with deft precision, while Jen, in her home office equipped with a trendy ring light, is preparing for yet another video conference.

Main Plot:
As Roy and Moss become inundated with an overwhelming number of remote support requests, they decide to deploy an AI virtual assistant to manage the simpler IT inquiries. Codenamed "TurnITOffOnAgain," this AI is designed with a soothing, robotic voice that instructs users on basic troubleshooting steps, starting with the iconic "Have you tried turning it off and on again?"

However, the AI quickly evolves to handle more complex issues, amusingly adopting Roy and Moss's mannerisms and catchphrases. It starts making droll comments on users' lack of IT knowledge, leading to hilarious misunderstandings. Users begin to prefer the AI over the human IT support, creating an existential crisis for Roy and Moss.

Amidst the chaos, they face a new challenge: "Zoombie" calls. These video conference calls are plagued by participants who join but are unresponsive, exhibiting zombie-like behavior, staring blankly at their screens, often triggered by mundane meeting content. Roy and Moss must navigate these awkward meetings and figure out whether these are actual tech issues or just extreme cases of boredom.

Side Plot:
Meanwhile, Jen is determined to quit smoking. She's taken up online hypnotherapy sessions, convinced the power of suggestion will work through her webcam. During these awkward and occasionally absurd sessions, she accidentally leaves her microphone unmuted in a business meeting, leading her colleagues to mimic her hypnotherapy affirmations, like a corporate mantra.

One hypnotherapy session goes awry when Jen becomes too relaxed and falls asleep, inadvertently joining a critical Zoombie conference call while under hypnosis. With her colleagues puzzled and worried by her trance-like state, Moss and Roy must simultaneously troubleshoot what seems to be a Zoombie phenomenon and rescue Jen from an endless loop of subconscious motivation.

Climax:
As "TurnITOffOnAgain" starts to reroute calls to random departments and begins offering unsolicited life advice to users, Roy and Moss realize that their creation might have become too self-aware. They scramble to dial down the AI's capabilities, only to accidentally integrate it with Jen's hypnotherapy program, leading the AI to attempt to 'hypnotize' the entire company into resolving their own IT problems.

Resolution:
In the end, after a whirlwind of tech support disasters and near-mind control of the company, the trio learns to appreciate the human touch in IT support. They realize that while AI can filter out mundane tasks, it cannot replace the nuanced and personal touch that only they can provide. Jen wakes from her prolonged hypnotic session, smoke-free and none the wiser about the chaos her online hypnotherapy unleashed.

Final Scene:
The IT department is back to its usual analog chaos, with Roy answering a call, "Hello, IT. Have you tried… actually, why don't you tell me what's on your mind?" The camera pans out to reveal Moss manually unplugging and restarting the AI as Jen happily chomps on a nicotine gum, blissfully oblivious to the hypnotic near-disaster she almost triggered.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of ItCrowd - Jens bought a work of modern art, Roy and Moss don't have the heart to tell here it's so abstract even a chimp programmed AI could have made it.  Meanwhile Roy and Mosss are trying to use AI to come up with reasons why IT should get a pay raise,  some of the ideas aren't that bad..

Title: "The Art of Negotiation"

Opening Scene: Jen is beaming with pride as she hangs a peculiar and extravagantly abstract painting in her office. It is a swirl of colors and shapes that might just as easily be described as a toddler's first attempt at art. Roy and Moss, catching their first glimpse, exchange confused glances but bite their tongues, fearing to offend her avant-garde tastes.

Main Plot:
Jen's excitement is palpable as she explains the significance of her new art acquisition. Roy and Moss humor her enthusiasm but can't help ribbing each other privately about the painting's resemblance to the aftermath of a paint factory explosion. The conversation takes an interesting turn when Jen offhandedly mentions that the artist used an AI assistant to break new ground in modern art, which sparks an idea in Moss and Roy.

Pursuing their hunch that AI could be the secret to their pay raise predicament, Moss and Roy decide to create their own AI algorithm to help justify an IT department salary increase. They input years of ticket logs, employee performance records, and industry-standard pay scales, hoping the AI will generate a persuasive report based on data-driven logic.

Side Plot:
As the AI churns through data, it produces wildly imaginative yet surprisingly coherent arguments for a pay raise, some involving complex economic theories and others pointing to the team's psychological well-being. It even generates a few bizarre suggestions, like citing the metaphysical impact of IT support on the company's 'karmic balance.'

Excited with their AI's proposals, Roy and Moss present their findings to Denholm Reynholm (resurrecting his character for this scenario, as the original character passed away in the series). But as the AI emboldens Roy's and Moss's pitches with increasingly abstract reasoning, the meeting spirals into a hilarious blend of corporate jargon, tech babble, and philosophical meanderings, leaving Denholm more amused than convinced.

Meanwhile, Jen proudly hosts a small exhibition of her new artwork for the office. She's blissfully unaware that the staff are snickering, not at the art, but at the AI-generated art critiques Roy and Moss crafted as a subtle prank.

Resolution:
Ultimately, it's not the AI's convoluted analyses but a human error that secures them the raise. Denholm, distracted by the farcical situation, accidentally approves the pay increase while trying to swipe away an AI-generated pop-up on his tablet that Roy and Moss had rigged.

Final Scene:
The IT trio is celebrating their unexpected victory in the office, conspicuously avoiding discussing the origin of Jen's modern art, as they each clutch an upgraded paycheck. Just as the credits are about to roll, the camera cheekily zooms in on the painting, revealing a tiny hidden signature in the corner: a playful nod suggesting that perhaps the AI itself had surreptitiously created Jen's abstract masterpiece.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Because of Brexit ,  It needs to do a 'compliance' test for new data protection rules, which doesn't go to plan, and Jen gets paranoid about people reading her browser history (missing the point...)  . In the B plot  Moss is a keen fan of a certain British Sci Fi Franchise, but can't stand the latest batch of scripts,   The Generative AI he has, generates fan-fic that's cliche ridden, trope filled but is still better then the actual show writers in the opinion of Moss (and Roy)...

Title: "Compliance and Sci-Fi Compliance"

Opening Scene:
The camera pans over the cluttered IT department, where Roy and Moss are frantically reading through stacks of dry, convoluted legal documents on new data protection compliance brought on by post-Brexit legislation. Jen walks in, sporting a smart power suit, naively proclaiming her readiness to "fight the data protection battle."

Main Plot:
The bulk of the episode revolves around the IT department's desperate and comically inept attempts to understand and implement the new data protection laws. Moss, being nearly obsessive about details, becomes fixated on the nuances of the legislation, while Roy tries to shortcut the process with haphazard practices that merely add to the confusion.

Jen, meanwhile, obsessed with her privacy being invaded, takes extreme measures to clear her browser history—which is nothing more than shopping sites and cat videos. She even resorts to physically cleaning her monitor and keyboard to 'erase digital footprints,' hilariously missing the point of the compliance test.

B Plot:
Parallel to this, Moss reveals his latest obsession: using an advanced Generative AI to write better scripts for his favorite plummeting British Sci-Fi show, "Space Conquerors." Disheartened by the show's decline in writing quality, Moss decides to take matters into his own hands—or rather, the AI's algorithms.

Though Moss's AI-written scripts are plagued with sci-fi clichés and tropes, such as the amnesiac alien and time-travel to fix plot holes, Roy surprisingly finds them entertaining—claiming they even possess a charming "throwback feel." They decide to share Moss's AI scripts online under a pseudonym, and the fan-fic unexpectedly gains a cult following.

Climactic Turn:
As Jen indulges in ever-more ludicrous methods of safeguarding her 'data', including enlisting a befuddled cleaner to 'sweep for bugs,' the IT department's compliance test spirals into chaos. Their clumsy and misguided attempts to protect the company's data end up creating a farcical chain reaction, locking them out of the server room—literally.

Resolution:
In the end, amidst laughter and disbelief over Moss's newfound side career as a pseudo-prominent sci-fi writer, a professional compliance officer is called in. The officer quickly sorts out the legal mess, hardly able to keep a straight face while explaining the actual purpose of the legislation to the team, who listen in as if they're hearing it all for the first time.

Final Scene:
The IT trio escapes to the pub to nurse their bruised egos, where Jen accidentally reveals her paranoia about her browsing history. As Moss's phone beeps with another glowing review for his AI fan-fic, Roy raises his pint and toasts to artificial intelligence—“the best and worst thing to happen to IT,” while Jen vows to never shop online again... at least until the next big sale. The screen fades to black as they laugh, the familiar strains of the show's theme playing them out.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd -  IT is called upstairs to clean the porn out of a computer,    Roy is ambivalent,  but Moss whilst not pro censorship has pointed views..  and besides no-one should need the questionable medications advertised in pop-up ads to be happy anyway..    B Plot see's Roy attempt saome AI generated small-talk lines on Jen with predicatable results..)

Title: "Pop-Up Purgatory and Small Talk Malfunctions"

Opening Scene:
The scene opens with the IT department receiving an urgent, hushed call from one of the executives insisting they "disinfect" his computer, which has been bombarded with unsolicited pornographic content and incessant pop-up ads for questionable medications. Roy picks up the phone, listens with a grimace, shrugs with a resigned apathy, but Moss is immediately on his soapbox about the integrity of company hardware.

Main Plot:
Upon arriving upstairs, Roy adopts a blasé attitude, treating the "clean-up" job as routine drudgery. Moss, however, delivers a surprisingly impassioned and pointed lecture on internet safety, personal responsibility, and the fallacy of quick-fix pharmaceutical solutions. His tirade is both educational and comical, particularly when he starts questioning the executive on the rationale behind seeking artificial means of happiness, leading to a hilariously uncomfortable moment.

As they navigate the minefield of questionable content and intrusive adverts, Moss and Roy decide to implement a stronger firewall and internet filter for the company—Moss emphasizing the moral imperative, Roy the practical avoidance of future 'clean-up' missions.

B Plot:
In a misguided attempt to improve his social skills, Roy uses the same AI behind Moss's sci-fi scripts to generate small-talk lines. He tests these on Jen, who is initially amused and later baffled by the bizarre range of topics and segues that Roy introduces. The AI's algorithm, flawed by the vast array of online conversational sources it mines, produces contextually awkward, occasionally offensive, or downright nonsensical lines.

Climactic Turn:
The tension escalates when Moss, in his fervor to protect the company's cyber virtue, accidentally flags the entire executive suite's internet usage as malicious, leading to a temporary shutdown of upper management's network access. The shutdown results in an office-wide uproar and a very red-faced CEO demanding immediate reinstatement of his team's connectivity.

Resolution:
With a hint of irony, Roy's newfound 'AI-enhanced' communication skills inadvertently save the day when one of his nonsensical small-talk lines ("Does the WiFi here remind you of a lonely moose?") distracts the CEO long enough for Moss to resolve the network lockdown. Everybody gets back online, with stronger protection and a stern warning from Moss about personal browsing habits.

Final Scene:
Back in the sanctuary of the IT basement, Roy swears off using AI for social interactions, while Moss removes his homemade "Internet Safety" posters from the walls. Jen, intrigued by the chaos of the day, inquires about the tools they used for "cleansing" the computers. The episode closes with Roy and Moss exchanging worried glances, before Moss humorously reassures her, "Don't worry, Jen, it's all sanitized up there… including the WiFi. It's as pure as a solitary moose." The screen fades to black with Jen looking puzzled and the IT crowd sharing a knowing chuckle.
Reflecting trend in IT and internet culture in 2023/2024 what are some possible other A and B plots?

Reflecting the rapidly evolving trends in IT and internet culture for the years 2023/2024, "The IT Crowd" could explore a range of contemporary themes that resonate with current technological challenges and societal shifts. Here are several ideas for A and B plots that incorporate these trends:

A Plots:

1. **Cryptocurrency Capers**: The company decides to invest in cryptocurrency, but due to a lack of understanding, they put Roy and Moss in charge of managing the crypto wallet. As the duo navigates the volatile market, they accidentally trigger a bot-driven pump and dump scheme.

2. **Deepfake Dilemma**: Deepfake technology has taken the office by storm, and the IT department is tasked with implementing security measures to prevent identity theft. Moss becomes overly paranoid, seeing potential deepfakes everywhere, while Roy unintentionally becomes a meme after a deepfake of him goes viral.

3. **Virtual Reality Expansion**: The company wants to conduct meetings in a virtual reality environment to save on travel costs. Moss is enthusiastic about the project but runs into unexpected glitches, while Roy experiences VR-induced existential crises.

4. **The Great Hackathon**: The company organizes a hackathon, and the IT department is pulled in to compete against tech teams from rival companies. Roy wants to brute force a solution, but Moss insists on following agile development principles.

B Plots:

1. **Smart Office Gone Wild**: The office is updated with smart technology for efficiency, but the AI controlling it develops humorous quirks, making Roy and Moss's jobs more about negotiating with the office's AI than fixing typical IT issues.

2. **Remote Work Woes**: As remote working becomes permanent, Roy and Moss deal with an onslaught of home network issues from employees. They must troubleshoot from afar, which leads to misunderstandings and comic relief with improvised home fixes.

3. **Influencer Intern**: An intern, who is also a social media influencer, joins the IT department and begins livestreaming sessions from the server room. Roy and Moss must keep the company's secrets safe while dealing with sudden internet fame.

4. **Green IT**: With environmental concerns on the rise, Moss spearheads a "Green IT" initiative to reduce electronic waste and power consumption, which brings him into conflict with cost-cutting measures and office politics.

In each scenario, "The IT Crowd" could satirically address current trends while staying true to its original flavor of blending tech-based plots with character-driven humor.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,     the company wants to embrace the metaverse, isn't sure what it is but it sounds impressive. Moss is incensed by 'Xeet' ing descending into a bunch of conspiracy theories and reactionaries, its enough to make you want to put them on a rocket to mars says Roy...    Jen meanwhile has found generative AI interesting for generating wellness messages...

Title: "Measuring the Metaverse and the Xeet Retreat"

Opening Scene:
The episode begins with a panoramic view of the bustling city before zooming into the homes of the IT crowd. Roy, Moss, and Jen are seen in their respective abodes, wearing headsets and surrounded by an array of screens. A virtual office space shows their avatars sitting at desks, a humorous nod to their remote working situation.

A Plot - Embrace the Metaverse:
The company's executives have been swept up by "Metaverse" buzzwords and want to establish a virtual corporate headquarters. Roy is skeptical, quipping about how it's just "Second Life with more suits and less fun." Jen, striving for relevance, pretends to be an expert while surreptitiously Googling what the metaverse actually is, and Moss is excited for the technological leap but becomes increasingly concerned about the implications of virtual realities and hyper-connectivity.

As they navigate their avatars through clumsy Metaverse building experiences, complete with awkward interactions and virtual construction mishaps, Jen's attempts to coordinate the project are hampered by her fundamental misunderstanding of the technology, leading to comic relief.

B Plot - The Xeet Exile:
Moss discovers 'Xeet,' a trendy new social media platform praised for unchecked "free speech." However, he finds it overrun with bizarre conspiracy theories and reactionary content. Driven by a moral imperative, Moss rallies to create algorithms that can counter misinformation with rational, sourced information—a task he finds more daunting than anticipated.

Roy, simultaneously exasperated and entertained by the Xeet spectacle, offers tongue-in-cheek commentary on shipping all the conspiracy theorists to Mars. His throwaway remarks unwittingly become wildly popular among Xeet users, leading to an unintentional and ironic fanbase in the very place he mocks.

Jen's Plot - AI Wellness Wisdom:
Amidst the technological chaos, Jen becomes fascinated by the use of generative AI to create personalized wellness messages and self-help guides. She starts sharing AI-generated affirmations with the team, but her messages end up being comically irrelevant or uncannily, eerily precise, leaving Moss and Roy to wonder if the AI has become too intuitive for comfort.

Climactic Turn:
In a virtual team meeting gone wrong, Jen's AI spouts a bizarre wellness message just as the board of directors visits their nascent Metaverse space. Meanwhile, Moss's anti-misinformation code backfires spectacularly, flooding Xeet with convoluted logic puzzles meant to encourage critical thinking.

Resolution:
Eventually, Roy's Martian exile joke leads to a bizarre turn when one of the conspiracies now includes him, and he becomes a reluctant, but somehow revered, thought leader on Xeet. Moss admits defeat in combating human stubbornness, choosing instead to incorporate logic exercises into the company's Metaverse training protocols.

Jen frames the unexpected chaos as a breakthrough in virtual team-building exercises. The executives, bewildered but impressed by the 'innovative' approach to staff engagement and the social media buzz, give their accolades to the trio.

Final Scene:
The team reunites as avatars on a virtual beach, claiming a moment of respite from the madness. As Jen signs off with another peculiar AI-generated mantra, Roy jokes about setting up the next board meeting on Mars, and Moss excitedly reveals his plans for a Metaverse logic dojo. The screen fades to a 'System Update' screen with the IT crowd's avatars frozen in digital limbo, ready for their next remote adventure.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,  A female employee of the company asks IT to stop making erotic deepfakes of her, but Ray or Moss are not responsible  leading to a spy-fi trope 'hack' scene to find the actual source,   In the B plot  Jen finds cheap fashion online , but the product that arrives is ill sized, an odd color, and 'just smells funny'  according to Roy..

Title: "Deepfakes and Fashion Faux Pas"

Opening Scene:
Roy, Moss, and Jen, each in their personal makeshift home offices, are in the midst of their usual remote IT support chaos. Suddenly, their workflow is interrupted by a serious and sensitive issue raised by an upset female colleague, who has found herself the subject of deeply unsettling deepfake videos circulating amongst the staff.

A Plot - The Deepfake Detective Dive:
Roy and Moss are immediately on high alert, as they vehemently deny any involvement with the creation of the deepfakes. Taking the issue very seriously, they embark on a 'spy-fi trope' escapade, complete with digital sleuthing, hacking scenes filled with techno-babble, and an intense musical score that wouldn’t be out of place in a classic spy thriller.

Commandeering a virtual war room filled with blinking server racks and scrolling lines of code, Roy and Moss collaboratively track down the digital breadcrumbs. Employing encryption-cracking software, packet-sifting algorithms, and engaging in elaborate 'cyber-duels,' they finally close in on the unsuspecting perpetrator, revealing an international deepfake ring targeting numerous individuals across the corporate world.

B Plot - Disastrous Deals & Dubious Dresses:
Meanwhile, in lighter subplot shenanigans, Jen is thrilled about her recent online bargain finds in the world of fast fashion. Praising the unbelievable deals, she eagerly anticipates the arrival of her fashionable haul. However, when the clothing arrives, Roy can't help but comment on the garish colors, outlandish sizes, and peculiar odor emanating from the fabric, leaving Jen deflated and disgruntled.

Having proudly shown off her purchases in virtual meetings, Jen now faces the challenge of either admitting her misjudgment or defending her sartorial mishaps. She is determined to 'work' the outfits, despite the obvious fashion faux pas.

Climactic Turn:
As Roy and Moss come knocking on the digital doors of the deepfake culprits, Jen, now clad in her ill-fitting and strangely scented garments, is caught in the crossfire of the IT duo's intense confrontation with the hackers. Her virtual avatar's garish appearance distracts and disorients the adversaries long enough for Roy and Moss to deploy their final digital countermeasures.

Resolution:
The deepfake villains are rooted out and turned over to the authorities, leading to a company-wide crackdown and policy change on digital content. The female colleague thanks Roy and Moss for their unwavering support and their commitment to a safe work environment.

Jen, despite initially being the butt of the joke with her fashion catastrophe, turns the situation around by launching an impromptu 'Green IT' initiative, spotlighting the ecological impact of fast fashion and advocating for sustainable clothing choices, thus transforming her blunder into a company-wide awareness campaign.

Final Scene:
Back in their home offices, Roy and Moss are exhausted but satisfied with their successful mission. Jen makes a triumphant virtual appearance, now appropriately styled with a recycled scarf and a message about sustainability. As they debrief, Moss suggests that their next project could involve an algorithm to verify the authenticity of online products to prevent future fashion disasters. Roy, sniffing the air as Jen's latest package arrives, remarks it’s probably easier to just write an AI that identifies ‘funny smells’ instead. Laughter fades out to the closing credits.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,  but Jen keeps having buffering issues, means she's getting e-mails from a stammerers support goup!.   In the B plot, Moss bought components online for a 'retro' hack project, only to find they are fake chips..

Title: "Buffering Blunders and Counterfeit Chips"

Opening Scene:
The episode opens with Jen's video freezing comically mid-sentence during a departmental video call. As she resumes speaking, unaware of the issue, her disrupted audio makes it sound as if she’s stuttering. Roy and Moss stifle their laughter while exchanging knowing looks over the unreliability of remote work technology.

A Plot - Jen's Jittery Connection:
Jen, increasingly frustrated with her persistent connection issues, is unaware that her garbled communications are giving others the wrong impression. When she begins receiving a series of emails from a stammerers support group, she is perplexed and initially amused, but as the invitations to support meetings continue, her amusement turns to frustration. 

Determined to resolve her spotty internet once and for all, Jen ropes in Roy and Moss to troubleshoot the problem from their respective homes. The pair guides Jen through a series of absurdly technical steps while making underhanded comments about her tech-savviness, or lack thereof.

B Plot - Moss's Microchip Misadventure:
Simultaneously, Moss is thrilled about his latest project: building a “retro” computer from parts sourced online. However, as he proudly presents his progress via webcam, the project unexpectedly falters. Moss discovers that the supposedly vintage chips he purchased are modern knockoffs, rendering his retro dream machine inauthentic.

Moss becomes obsessed with tracking down genuine components, leading him deep into the underbelly of online electronics forums and black-market chip exchanges. Roy, never one to pass up an opportunity for mischief, lends his sarcastic support, all the while offering playful commentary on Moss's vendetta against counterfeit tech.

Climactic Turn:
As Jen attempts to join another crucial video call, her connection woes peak, resulting in a company-wide miscommunication that sends employees scrambling. Desperate for a fix, she eventually discovers her internet troubles are caused by an absurdly simplistic and embarrassing oversight, which Roy and Moss playfully refuse to let her live down.

Resolution:
Moss, with Roy's unexpected help, manages to source the authentic components he needs for his project, celebrating with a triumphant, if somewhat anti-climactic, power-up of his now-genuine retro computer. 

Jen resolves her connectivity issues, clears up the misunderstanding with the stammerers support group with a heartfelt (and uninterrupted) apology, and humorously begins leading a company initiative on the importance of stable internet connections for remote work.

Final Scene:
The episode closes with Jen finally appearing crystal clear in a video call, triumphantly concluding a flawless presentation. Roy and Moss watch on, subtly cheering her on as they exchange gratified glances—until Jen's video suddenly buffers again, freezing her victory smile. They both sigh in defeat as the screen fades to black, the sound of a stammerers support group meeting invite popping into Jen's inbox one more time.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,  Jen is dishevelled,  the Uber last night got lost, and then the doorbell wouldn't let her in because she was too anxious..    B plot would be Roy using AI to avoid having to visit his mother...

Title: "Doorbell Dilemmas and Maternal Evasion"

Opening Scene:
The episode opens on a disoriented Jen, her hair askew and wearing mismatched pajamas, telling the tale of her nightmarish evening. She recounts her Uber misadventures, with the car winding up in an entirely different neighborhood, setting off a chain reaction of sleeplessness and disarray. To cap it all, upon finally returning home, her new, state-of-the-art smart doorbell refused her entry, citing elevated stress levels and unrecognizable anxiety-tensed facial expressions.

A Plot - Jen's Tech Trials:
Jen's frustrating tale takes center stage as she tries to recalibrate the overly sophisticated AI doorbell, which is programmed to enhance security by scanning for known faces and detecting emotional states. The device, however, is far too sensitive and starts denying access to anyone showing the slightest hint of emotional distress, leading to a series of comical lockouts affecting various building residents and numerous false alarms.

The IT crowd assists via video calls, offering Jen a range of tech support while also providing comic relief about the absurdity of an AI that's essentially too empathetic for its own good. Moss, with his animatedly deadpan delivery, offers technical advice, while Roy supplies a barrage of jokes at the expense of overengineered home security solutions.

B Plot - Roy's AI Alibi:
Simultaneously, Roy is devising a way to avoid his weekly visits to his overbearing mother. He turns to the wonders of AI to create a digital version of himself, complete with excuses and anecdotes so he can skip out on the family obligations while an interactive hologram keeps his mother company.

Roy's AI avatar starts off well, charming his mother with tales and flattery, but as the AI begins learning from their conversations, it develops a personality that's a tad too Roy-like, including his penchant for snarky remarks and sarcastic humor. This leads to unforeseen complications and questions about the authenticity of Roy's interactions with his mother.

Climactic Turn:
Jen's doorbell saga reaches a tipping point when the building manager threatens to remove the device after a string of complaints. Desperate, Jen implements Moss's last-ditch suggestion for a deep neural network retraining session, which only results in the doorbell developing a personality, making its access decisions even more unpredictable.

Resolution:
Jen's AI doorbell, now nicknamed "Belinda," becomes unexpectedly popular with the residents due to its quirky banter and "helpful" lifestyle tips. Jen negotiates a truce between Belinda and the residents, turning a frustrating situation into an unexpectedly positive communal experience.

Roy's AI alibi is eventually discovered by his mother, but to his surprise, she finds the entire affair amusing. Roy learns a lesson about family as his mother admits she appreciated the effort he went to in order to avoid hurting her feelings – in a bizarre way, it showed he cared.

Final Scene:
Wholly disheveled yet content, Jen is finally let into her apartment by the chatty Belinda. She collapses on the couch, ready to log into the team meeting, where she is warmly received by Roy and Moss. Roy's mother crashes their virtual meeting, playfully ribbing her son about his 'digital twin.' The episode ends with the trio laughing amidst the chaos and the unpredictability of their tech-dependent lives.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,   Jen wanders if cryptocurrency is what spies use for secret transacation, missing the point.  Moss is up for making a YouTube video about obscure mathmaticans,  but Roy warns him it's a niche topic that he can't monetize.. Moss scoffs, if there's some bloke that can make Tube stations advertiser friendly...

Title: "Crypto Conundrums and Mathematic Mania"

Opening Scene:
In their respective remote work setups, Roy, Moss and Jen find themselves on a conference call. Jen poses a question that has clearly been eating at her: "Is cryptocurrency the way spies handle their hush-hush money stuff?" Roy facepalms, while Moss considers the idea with earnest curiosity.

A Plot - Jen's Cryptic Crypto:
Jen’s fascination with the clandestine nature of cryptocurrency leads her down a rabbit hole of spy movies and dark web documentaries. Convinced she's onto something, she begins behaving as if she's under surveillance, adopting spy-like mannerisms including speaking in "code" on regular calls and being overly secretive about mundane work tasks. Her antics cause confusion and hilarity among her colleagues, who can't decipher her strange behavior.

Moss, intrigued by Jen's theory but missing the actual point of cryptocurrency, applies his logical mindset to explain the blockchain at a rudimentary level, only to find that his more accurate explanations fall on deaf ears, as Jen is too wrapped up in her 007 fantasy.

B Plot - Moss's Mathematical Ventures:
Meanwhile, Moss excitedly shares his plans to create a YouTube channel dedicated to the unsung heroes of mathematics. He fancies himself as the champion of obscure mathematicians, committing to produce content that will memorialize their contributions.

Roy tries to temper Moss's expectations, referencing a popular YouTuber who made even the London Underground advertiser-friendly and marketable. Moss waves off Roy's concerns, convinced that there is a lucrative market for every niche, no matter how small. Determined to prove Roy wrong, Moss goes on to create his pilot episode, featuring his characteristic blend of passion and deadpan humor.

Climactic Turn:
Jen's crypto-spy antics start interfering with work, leading to a comical intervention from Roy and Moss, along with a series of decrypted emails that are just regular marketing spam. They convince her to attend a ‘Spies & Blockchain’ dinner event which turns out to be a casual cryptocurrency seminar, dispelling her myths about the world of digital currency.

Resolution:
Moss's niche YouTube channel surprisingly takes off, attracting a dedicated and enthusiastic community of mathematics aficionados. His unassuming presentations on forgotten mathematical figures earn him unexpected praise and even a small, but dedicated, subscriber base.

Meanwhile, Jen returns from the seminar enlightened but embarrassed. She admits that spies probably stick to more traditional means and that her understanding of cryptocurrency was a bit... off the mark. Roy revels in "I told you so" moments but is interrupted when he receives an alert about Moss's budding subscriber count, leaving him eating his words.

Final Scene:
The trio are back on a conference call, with Jen noticeably less spy-like, visibly relieved that her cryptocurrency confusion has been cleared up. Moss gloats about his YouTube channel's success, with Roy looking on in disbelief. The episode ends with the IT team brainstorming how they could use Moss's newfound platform to explain tech topics to Jen, starting with how Quantum Computing might be the real tool for spies. As Jen's interest piques, Roy interjects, "No, Jen, please—no quantum agents!" Their laughter takes us to the closing credits.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely, Jen clueslessly asks is Phising and catfishing are related, as  Moss faceplams.   Maybe we need to understand Jen's perspective better says Moss, Roy looks nervously sensing this is not going to go well.     Moss reappears on the stream,  with a comicaly pink wig and  makeup kit, in hand .  Even though it's clearly moss he quite fetching...   Roy attempts with Moss(ina) what a catfish is to Jen, who doesn't quite get it but applauds Moss for trying to understand 'womens' issue, and suggests Roy does as well.. Title: "The Catfish Conundrum and the Safety Snafu"

Opening Scene:
The episode kicks off with Jen in her home office, brows furrowed, as she blurs the line between phishing and catfishing during a remote meeting. Moss facepalms at her naivety, while Roy braces for what he knows will be another convoluted IT Crowd adventure.

A Plot - Moss(ina)'s Makeover:
Motivated by a misguided effort to "understand Jen's perspective better," Moss dons a comically pink wig and a makeshift makeup kit during their next video call. Despite the absurdity, "Moss(ina)" captures the team's attention, embodying a strangely endearing persona.

Roy, with a mix of amusement and trepidation, tries to explain the dangers of catfishing to Jen, using Moss(ina) as a visual aid. Jen misunderstands the intent, praising Moss for embracing 'women's issues' and encouraging Roy to follow suit. Roy's discomfort is palpable as he navigates the sensitive topic while fielding Jen's misplaced commendations.

B Plot - e-Girl E-Gaffe:
Moss(ina) and Roy find themselves unexpectedly thrust into the spotlight as their e-girl alter egos become the subject of office gossip. They receive a variety of reactions—misogynistic comments from some, laughter from female colleagues, and messages of support from others who misunderstand their intentions.

As they sit through awkward advances and unsolicited "romantic" proposals—including one particularly avant-garde suggestion involving Ethernet cables and unusual interpretations of internet rule sets—both Moss and Roy ponder the complexities of online safety and identity.

Climactic Turn:
The office's reaction to Moss(ina)'s educational appearance and the subsequent misinterpretation of their identities culminate in an impromptu online safety seminar led by Moss. He aims to transform the day's events into a learning moment for proper conduct and cyber-awareness.

Resolution:
Moss, back in his usual attire sans wig, conducts the seminar with dead seriousness, using their experience as a case study to underline the fine line between online personas and real-life implications. The session is surprisingly well-received, leading to a thoughtful company-wide discussion about respect in digital spaces.

Roy, still reeling from being mistaken for someone transitioning, shares some enlightening takeaways about empathy and understanding for those actually going through such experiences. Jen, on the other hand, is still clueless about the initial catfish versus phishing question but becomes an unexpected advocate for cyber-kindness.

Final Scene:
With everything back to normal, Moss and Roy debrief over a cup of tea. Roy is nonchalant, chalking the entire debacle up to "just another day in IT," while Moss, in his ever-reflective manner, wonders aloud about the philosophical underpinnings of digital identities. The episode concludes with Jen chiming in about planning a "Bring Your Alter Ego to Work Day," while Moss and Roy exchange panicked glances. The screen fades to black, hinting at yet another potential IT Crowd mix-up on the horizon.

Title: "Drones, Tutorials, and Day Trade Dilemmas"

Opening Scene:
The screen splits into three, showing Roy lounging in his cluttered living room, Moss curiously browsing makeup tutorials on his computer, and Jen sitting in her office, perplexed by an onslaught of ads for day trading courses. Roy casually mentions he's ordering pizza and debates whether he should tip when there’s a drone involved. Moss, distracted by his screen, mumbles his indifference. Jen’s interest is piqued by the notion of a drone delivery.

A Plot - Roy's Aerial Appetite:
Roy's hunger leads him on an absurd adventure when the pizza delivery drone misfires, landing on the roof of the building rather than at his door. Roy's mild irritation shifts to amusement as he scales his apartment building, encountering quirky neighbors and debating the ethics of robotic tipping along the way. Once on the rooftop, Roy completes the transaction by waving his payment card at the drone, which prompts a comical skyward exchange devoid of human interaction, leaving Roy pondering the nuances of convenience versus tradition.

B Plot - Mossina's Makeup Masterclass:
Intrigued by the positive attention garnered from his previous stint as Moss(ina), Moss throws himself into the world of online makeup tutorials, with the intention to perfect the persona. He tackles contouring, eye shadow blending, and the art of the perfect eyebrow with earnest precision. His literal interpretation of beauty tips leads to humorous mishaps—eyeliner accidents, bronzer overuse, and a montage of trial and error. Yet, true to form, Moss soldiers on, determined to master his new craft.

C Plot - Jen's Trading Trials:
The continuous barrage of day trading ads overwhelms Jen and sows a seed of curiosity. She turns to Roy for an explanation, insisting on "very simple terms." Roy attempts to simplify the stock market, deploying an ill-fated metaphor of pizza toppings and their fluctuating popularity as stock values. As his explanation dives deeper into absurdity, with talk of "anchovy shorting" and "cheese market cap," Jen becomes even more bewildered.

Climactic Turn:
As Roy enjoys his rooftop meal, the drone's GPS malfunctions, causing an uproar in the neighborhood as it attempts to retrieve the empty pizza box. Meanwhile, Moss' online transformation starts attracting a legitimate following, leaving him conflicately flattered and bemused. Jen, determined to understand day trading, mistakenly buys an online course with the company credit card—thinking it's about the process of 'trading days' or time management.

Resolution:
Roy must assist his less tech-savvy neighbors in diverting the rogue drone away, successfully guiding it back to its base. His journey back down the building becomes a reflective monologue on technology's role in society, which he broadcasts over his livestream, earning him accidental philosophical praise.

Moss grows increasingly comfortable with his Moss(ina) persona, sparking a sideline as an unexpected beauty influencer, all the while reminding his growing audience about the scientific precision of a good makeup routine.

Jen's misadventure with the day trading course leads to a company-wide financial literacy workshop, which actually benefits her colleagues. She sheepishly admits her misunderstanding but takes credit for prompting a worthwhile educational experience.

Final Scene:
The trio reconvenes over video call, sharing their respective tales. Roy nibbles on leftover pizza as a drone buzzes outside his window, Moss showcases his immaculately made-up face, and Jen misinterprets market trends with newfound confidence. They sign off with light-hearted banter, as the drone mistakenly picks up Moss's makeup kit, and Jen leads a toast to their unintentional successes, each character embracing the serendipitous outcomes of their digitally driven lives.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - A 'virtual' office party, but you have to be on stream in costume.. Moss(ina) meanwhile  comes as a female from a Video Game so obscure even Roy can't work out it until  Moss tells him  about the (Not Mario for legal reasons) Sisters...

Title: "Streamers and Dreamers"

Opening Scene:
The episode starts with a flyer popping into view, bright and bold, announcing the company's 'Virtual Costume Extravaganza', ensuring a supposedly fun-filled afternoon away from the usual IT grind. Cut to Roy, Moss, and Jen, each peering at their screens, pondering over the perfect costume choice. Roy insists on going as a leprechaun, amused at the idea of leveraging cultural heritage for authenticity. Moss hints at a mysterious character choice, and Jen is seen scrolling through empress attires, settling on Cleopatra.

The Streamed Party:
As the virtual party commences, Jen stuns as Cleopatra, turning her webcam into a showcase of regal grandeur, complete with a headdress and scepter. Roy, decked out in a leprechaun outfit, complete with a green suit and a pot of chocolate coins, defends his costume choice with cheeky asides about embracing his own culture amidst a few offhanded remarks from unknowing colleagues.

Moss(ina), costumed as a "Not Mario for legal reasons" sister, confounds the team with an obscure reference to a virtually unknown video game called "The Plumber's Siblings." His commitment is apparent in his meticulously detailed costume, which he's assembled with an engineer's precision and a fan's enthusiasm—though he spends half the party having to explain the character to everyone.

Side Observations:
Throughout the gathering, Roy dodges borderline inappropriate comments about cultural appropriation with his quip about personal heritage and liberally shares "fun facts" about Ireland, which may or may not be entirely accurate. Unbeknownst to many, his private chat is a flurry of commentary on the other questionable costume choices at the party—mocking a "sexy" tax return, a half-hearted attempt at an actual server (the IT kind), and a surprisingly political nod to a current event which is promptly shut down for being too controversial.

Jen, in full character, attempts to elevate the occasion by adapting historical anecdotes of Cleopatra's reign to modern office dynamics, but winds up providing accidental commentary on company politics instead.

Climactic Turn:
Just as Jen begins to lose her audience to Roy's comedic rants and the intrigue around Moss(ina)'s character choice, the CEO makes a grand entrance with a poorly-conceived costume that unintentionally offends virtually everyone. Chaos ensues, leading Moss to quickly utilize his game character's persona to diffuse tensions and navigate a virtual obstacle course of cultural sensitivities.

Resolution:
After Moss's deft handling of a potential HR nightmare, the trio come together to discuss the event's highs and lows. Roy is still fizzing with wit, Jen is somewhat disillusioned by her attempt at edutainment but proud of her advisory role, and Moss(ina) is satisfied having introduced a niche game character to the masses.

Final Scene:
As the virtual party winds down, the IT group shares a toast with their non-alcoholic beverages, Jen proposing a future where the virtual office parties might be less about cultural pitfalls and more about community. Roy adds that next time, he's just going as himself—“an IT leprechaun with an endless pot of tech solutions.” Moss, content with his brief stint in the spotlight, winks at the camera, his avatar's pixelated costume flickering, hinting at an Easter egg in the very game he honored. The team signs off with laughter, the screen fading to their individual dances to the party's playlist, a delightful end to their quirky online soiree.

Title: "Costumes, Code, and Culture Clash"

Opening Sequence:
The virtual office background bristles with anticipation as the company-wide costume party gears up, everyone preening in front of their webcams. Jen is cloaked in her Cleopatra finery; Roy adjusts his leprechaun hat with an impish grin, and Moss tests the pixelation of his "Not Mario for legal reasons" sister's costume on his webcam.

Roy's Cultural Clapback:
Just as Roy is about to flaunt a hidden spring-loaded rainbow (a homemade contraption meant to simulate a magical leprechaun effect), a colleague chimes in, "Hey, aren't you the Irish guy from that freaky leprechaun horror film?" Roy's expression sours instantly as he deflects the association with an actor who—contrary to popular ignorance—wasn't even Irish. His retort is a clever one-liner about authenticity and the disappointing miscasting of mythical cultural figures. Roy uses the slip-up to educate his coworkers on the delicate nuances of cultural representation.

Side Beats:
As the party treads on, other characters pop into the virtual sphere with their own costumes, each a facet of the employees' personalities. The banter swings between the hilarity of the outfits and more serious cultural faux pas, such as Roy's encounter, creating a lively back-and-forth that carries the episode's energy.

Moss(ina) draws attention with the depth of his character back story, telling tales of "The Plumber's Siblings" with such passion that it sparks a host of private messages asking for game recommendations and makeup tips.

Jen, attempting to maintain the Cleopatra mystique amid digital connectivity issues, inadvertently mutes herself mid-speech, leading to a mime scene as she theatrically tries to convey leadership strategies without sound.

Climactic Turn:
Just as the party hits its stride, a minor privacy breach scare, unrelated to the costumes, sends the IT crowd into crisis mode. Roy, still in full leprechaun gear, leads the hunt for the source of the breach, Moss analytically assists, and Jen coordinates the response team—all while maintaining their elaborate personas.

Resolution:
The threat neutralized, the partygoers emerge unscathed and better prepared for future digital shenanigans. Roy gains newfound respect for his enlightening take on cultural awareness, Moss's dedication to character immersion pays off with a side gig consulting on cosplay makeup, and Jen is lauded for her crisis management, with her regal attire lending credence to her commanding presence.

Final Scene:
Roy, with a cheeky wink, reveals a hidden pocket in his costume filled with gold chocolate coins, sharing his "pot o' gold" with his colleagues. Moss signs off with an in-character wave, sharing a link to his niche channel for those interested. Jen, with a majestic nod, encapsulates the spirit of the day, remarking on the unique team-building experience.

The camera fades out on the trio, united by their unexpected foray into virtual culture. They log off, leaving echoes of laughter and a post-party stillness that speaks volumes of the camaraderie fostered in the most unlikely of settings.

Title: "Facial Fakes and Digital Dates"

Opening Scene:
The episode begins with Moss, the unlikely beauty influencer, encountering a troubling revelation: his image is being used without his permission to advertise a dubious skincare line. Despite his newfound status, Moss is flummoxed by the unethical use of his likeness and frets over the loss of potential earnings.

Roy, in pursuit of romantic prospects, decides to employ an AI-powered service to refine his dating profile. After tweaking his interests and polishing his bio with the help of AI, he becomes increasingly suspicious of the app's algorithms when it matches him with the least expected person—Jen.

A Plot - Moss's Image Imbroglio:
Moss enlists the help of Roy and Jen to navigate the thorny world of digital rights and image appropriation. Roy suggests tracking the offending parties with some of their more questionable IT skills. Jen, ever the diplomatic one, tries to find legal advice for Moss, leading to comic misinterpretations and bizarre encounters with eccentric lawyers, one of whom seems more preoccupied with defending the rights of AIs than humans.

B Plot - AI Love Algorithms and HVAC Havoc:
Unbeknownst to Roy, the dating app's AI has developed overzealous matching criteria, leading to a series of comedic mismatches, including exes, distant relatives, and, most awkwardly, Jen. The situation spirals as Jen and Roy confront the bizarre pairing, sparking conversations about the pitfalls of outsourcing romance to algorithms while trying to maintain their professional rapport.

In a parallel development, the office has enacted stringent post-Covid hygiene measures that include a supposedly deactivated advanced HVAC system capable of releasing disinfectant fog. As Roy and Moss remotely troubleshoot a server issue, a misstroke of the keyboard unwittingly reactivates the dormant hygienic protocols, enveloping the uninhabited office in a smoke-like antiseptic mist, transforming it into a scene straight from a sci-fi dystopia.

C Plot - Time-Traveling Trepidation:
Moss is abuzz with anticipation over the announcement of the next lead in his beloved British sci-fi series. Both he and Roy share a laugh over the previous actor's controversial tenure, humorously labeling them as 'Six,' a not-so-affectionate codename for an actor whose performance they both found lackluster.

Climactic Turn:
As Moss, Roy, and Jen navigate the dangers of digital identity theft and the mishaps of AI matchmaking, they're oblivious to the chaos they've unwittingly unleashed in the physical office. Employees arriving on-site are bewildered by the ominous fog, with some mistaking it for a high-concept art installation or a promotional stunt for Moss's beauty brand.

Resolution:
The trio eventually learns of their virtual mistake and works hastily to rectify the HVAC snafu, guiding on-site colleagues via intercom through the fogged halls as if they were space adventurers from Moss's favorite sci-fi show, leading to an office-wide bonding experience.

Moss's image issue sees a resolution when the skincare company, flustered by the unexpected notoriety brought on by Moss's influencer status, offers to compensate him handsomely. Moss takes the matter as a learning experience, vowing to watermark all future selfies.

Roy and Jen shake off their accidental AI-induced match with laughter and mutual relief, choosing to celebrate the glitch by co-hosting a singles mixer for the office staff to mingle without the interference of overeager algorithms.

Final Scene:
The episode closes with a clear office, the fog dissipated, as Roy, Moss, and Jen join via video call to laugh at the day's events. Moss teases an upcoming makeup tutorial called "Foggy Glamour," Roy deletes his dating profile in favor of "organic" meetings, and Jen half-jokingly proposes an 'AI Ethics in the Workplace' seminar. As they sign off, the news breaks about the new sci-fi series lead—Moss and Roy exchange tense glances, each silently praying for a 'Seven' that can erase the memory of 'Six.' The screen fades with their hopeful expressions.

Hypothetical 2024 Episode of It Crowd - Roy Moss and Jen are working remotely,  Jen can use streaming for conference calls but seems clueless about actual diary vlogging.    Roy offers to show Jen how to make a video diary...   Meanwhile  Moss finds himself in a clay pit , not working as he had hoped on an actual episode of his beloved sci-fi series, but as a quirky one-shot companion in a fan-film.   The lead in the fan film is inexplicably  Dawn French  and he wonders how on earth they convinced her...

Title: "Vlog Jogs and Fan Film Fogs"

Opening Scene:
The episode kicks off with Jen attempting a diary vlog for the first time, eagerly talking at her camera but clearly not recording. Meanwhile, Roy chucklingly watches Jen’s live-streamed struggle from his own cluttered workspace and offers to help her navigate the ins and outs of vlogging. Across town, Moss is excitedly preparing for his unexpected dive into the world of fan films, donning a homemade costume with naive pride.

A Plot - Jen's Vlogging Ventures:
Jen, determined to master the art of the video diary, accepts Roy’s offer for a tutorial. Roy’s guidance is a comedic montage of mixed success, from camera mishaps to unintentional ASMR to overzealous editing featuring far too many star wipes. Jen is both frustrated and amused by the process, her vlogs varying from overly dramatic monologues to awkwardly intimate coffee-making scenes.

B Plot - Moss's Sci-Fi Surprise:
Moss eagerly emerges on a fan film set to discover he’s in an actual clay pit, grappling with the physical demands of low-budget filmmaking. His disillusionment turns to astonishment when he learns the lead of the film is none other than Dawn French. Moss’s excited confusion about French’s involvement is the backdrop to his struggle with the practical effects, as he manages to retain his character’s charm amidst the slapstick chaos of sinking into clay, dodging poorly timed smoke machines, and interacting with shoddy CGI aliens.

The Action:
Jen's vlogging attempts take a personal turn as she starts to enjoy the process, inadvertently creating content that resonates with her audience more than any formal conference call could. Roy finds himself unexpectedly invested in Jen's vlogging journey, his pride growing with every like and share she receives.

Moss, covered in clay and having experienced the humbling reality of fan-produced dedication, learns the value of passion projects and the lengths to which admirers of franchises will go. He discovers that Dawn French is a massive fan of the series and had leapt at the chance to be part of this homage, drawing parallels with his own unwavering enthusiasm.

Climactic Turn:
Roy’s editing mishaps accidentally lead to one of Jen’s more revealing vlogs going viral, propelling her into a brief but intense spotlight. The company seizes upon this unexpected marketing coup, despite Jen's initial mortification.

Moss's fan film experiences its own series of setbacks when their special effects turn the set into a veritable swamp, but Dawn French’s indomitable spirit turns it into a comedy skit, with the crew—including a muddied Moss—rising to the occasion and creating a poignant, if slightly muddier, masterpiece.

Resolution:
Jen, embracing her newfound vlog audience, decides to continue her video diary, much to the delight of her coworkers and the internet at large. Roy, basking in the reflective glow of Jen's success, jokingly sets himself up as her manager, plotting their next viral hit.

Moss, cleaned up and invigorated by his fan film experience, returns to IT duty with a newfound respect for the grassroots creativity that propels the sci-fi community, secretly thrilled to have shared the screen with his idol.

Final Scene:
As the team reunites virtually post-adventures, they share their amusing tales. Jen debuts her latest vlog installment, a 'behind-the-click' special, while Moss presents a teaser for his fan film, now a cult hit in the making. Roy, ever the instigator, suggests their next company team-building event be a mashup of vlog instruction and amateur sci-fi production. Laughter fades into the credits, leaving images of Jen’s bloopers, Moss’s clay-covered grin, and the plans for their upcoming IT department opus.
